Details: Control by chlorine can be effective for some degradates. Data from one study reported variable removal (2 to 100 percent) of metolachlor degradates by chlorine. The results suggest that removal is dependent on the compound's functional group. For the conditions tested, chlorine was effective for degradates lacking an acetanilide functional group. Hydroxymetolachlor (8%), deschlorometolachlor (2%), metolachlor morpholinone (13%) and metolachlor propanol (7%) were removed less by chlorine in comparison to deschloroacetyl metolachlor(100%) and deschloroacetyl metolachlor propanol (100%).
